key_insight: A label-agnostic self-supervised learning pipeline can discover clinically interpretable tibial plateau fracture phenotypes directly from radiographs, without relying on inconsistent human labels.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reproducible way to build fracture representations that may complement traditional Schatzker/AO-OTA classification and support more robust downstream decision tools.
method: Cleaned 154 knee radiographs and fine-tuned a RadImageNet-pretrained ResNet-50 with SimCLR.
Reduced embeddings with UMAP and clustered them with k-means into four imaging-derived phenotypes.
Validated phenotypes using blinded review from two independent clinicians.
evidence_signal: The four clusters showed moderate stability and strong cohesion (bootstrap ARI 0.319±0.041, silhouette 0.511), with expert coherence ratings of 3–5/5 and one cluster unanimously recognized as comminuted.
watch_item: The dataset is small and single-task, so cluster robustness and clinical generalizability need confirmation on larger, multi-center cohorts.
paper_title: Phenotyping TPF via Self-Supervised Learning: A Label-Agnostic Framework with Expert Validation
